title: Pseudo-mutual gazing enhances interbrain synchrony during remote joint attention tasking.

assets.abstract: INTRODUCTION: Mutual gaze enables people to share attention and increase engagement during social interactions through intentional and implicit messages. Although previous studies have explored gaze behaviors and neural mechanisms underlying in-person eye contact, the growing prevalence of remote communication has raised questions about how to establish mutual gaze remotely and how the brains of interacting individuals synchronize. METHODS: To address these questions, we conducted a study using
